justification: All pass criteria met: 14 failure modes identified (well above 5 minimum); phantom charge risk explicitly covered in F4, F6, F7 with idempotency keys and saga compensation; fire-and-forget notification weakness addressed with transactional outbox (Layer 6); circuit breaker (Layer 4) and timeout budgets (Layer 1) recommended with specific parameters; idempotency keys specified for payment retries (Layer 3) with Stripe native support; trade-offs table acknowledges added complexity for each resilience pattern.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failure enumeration — 14 modes across 3 categories (sync chain, fire-and-forget, systemic), well beyond the 5 minimum
Phantom charge covered from multiple angles: network partition (F4), crash after payment (F6), and timeout mid-charge (F7), each with targeted resilience
Transactional outbox pattern is the correct architectural fix for fire-and-forget — not just 'add retries' but a fundamental pattern change with at-least-once delivery guarantee
Retry policy correctly constrained: only safe because of idempotency keys, only for transient errors (429, 503), NOT for business errors (400, 402, 422)
Specific implementation parameters provided: 3 retries, 200ms base delay, 2s max, full jitter; timeout budgets cascading inward (10s → 8s → 5s); circuit breaker thresholds
Saga pattern with compensation addresses the 'charged but no order record' failure mode — persist order BEFORE payment call
Implementation guidance orders layers by dependency (timeouts first, then idempotency keys as prerequisite for safe retries)
Distinguishes transient vs permanent failures in retry policy — business errors are not retried
Trade-offs are concrete and well-reasoned, including alternatives considered for each decision

agent_output_excerpt:
# Failure Mode Analysis & Resilience Architecture

## Executive Summary

This architecture has a critical synchronous dependency chain (Order → Payment → Stripe) that creates cascading failure risk, plus a fire-and-forget notification path that silently loses customer emails. The resilience design introduces circuit breakers, retries with backoff, idempotency keys, a transactional outbox for notifications, and timeout budgets to convert hard failures into graceful degradation.

---

## Architect
